label: Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms

node.label: Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms
node.definition: Signals are transformed into frequency or basis domains where global structure can be modeled efficiently.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms is to connect Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Linear algebra to downstream capabilities including Structured state-space models (S4 family), Neural operators, FNOs & PINNs.
examples: substrate for Structured state-space models (S4 family)
substrate for Neural operators, FNOs & PINNs
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Signal processing & harmonic analysis, Linear algebra.

dependencies.rationale: Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms uses Signal processing & harmonic analysis as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms uses Linear algebra as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Fourier, wavelet & spectral transforms uses Numerical analysis as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
